content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as cumulative curiosity ecosystems, where each stored memory fragment acts as an independent micro-agent that actively votes on which new unlabeled data the parent agent should acquire next. Micro-agents that consistently identify high-impact data that improves overall task performance are reinforced and allocated more memory storage, while low-contribution micro-agents are pruned to free up space.
